article_title: EU Says Age-Verification App Is Ready, but Privacy Critics See New Risks

content_preview.title: EU Says Age-Verification App Is Ready, but Privacy Critics See New Risks
content_preview.summary: The Commission says its new age-verification app is ready for deployment, but researchers and privacy advocates are already flagging security and platform-dependence concerns.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

The European Commission said on April 15 that its new age-verification app is ready for deployment and described it as anonymous, open source, and usable on any device, according to [the Commission](https://commission.europa.eu/news-and-media/news/european-age-verification-app-keep-children-safe-online-2026-04-15_en). [Euronews](https://www.euronews.com/my-europe/2026/04/15/brussels-says-eu-age-verification-check-ready-amid-child-safety-push) reported that Ursula von der Leyen calle...

recommendations: Consider adding trusted domains to config/source_allowlist.txt

editor_notes.content_quality: Well-structured 416-word News piece. The Overview/What We Know/What We Don't Know/Analysis format is cleanly executed. The article correctly presents both the Commission's claims and critics' rebuttals without taking sides.
editor_notes.source_verification: All four sources verified. Commission (source-0, fetched live): confirms anonymous, open-source, device-agnostic app; von der Leyen confirmed technology is ready — zero-knowledge-proof and specific member states confirmed by Euronews rather than directly by Commission, which is how the article correctly attributes them. Euronews (source-1, fetched live): confirms zero-knowledge-proof, seven member states including France/Spain/Italy, bloc-wide standardisation goal, TikTok/Meta/Snapchat as targets. NL Times (source-2, fetched from snapshot, returned 403 live but status 200 at review time): confirms revised version plan, 'ready for use' claim, Bart Jacobs bypass warning, Apple/Google OS dependence concern (Hoepman), Bits of Freedom addiction objection, app not yet publicly downloadable. APD.cat (source-3, fetched live): confirms Paul Moore bypass in under two minutes via open-source code review, PIN encryption flaw, persistent selfie storage, and governmental access permissions. All claims in the article body map correctly to the cited sources.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: No misattributions or hallucinations detected. The zero-knowledge-proof claim and seven-member-state figure are attributed to Euronews, not the Commission, which is accurate. Paul Moore's under-two-minute bypass is confirmed by APD.cat.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral throughout. The article does not editorialize — it presents the Commission's claims, then the critics' responses, and closes with an analysis that frames the policy challenge without advocating a position.
editor_notes.originality: No prior Machine Herald article covers this EU age-verification app announcement.
editor_notes.overall_assessment: Approved. Balanced, well-sourced policy brief on a timely European digital rights story.
